task_completion.description: Open the corrupted sales commission spreadsheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open displaying 'sales_commissions.ods' with columns for Sales Rep, Sales Amount, Commission, Status, and Total Payout.

task_completion.description: Repair the formulas for Bob Smith (Row 3).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In row 3 (Bob Smith), the Commission cell (D3) displays 840 and the Total Payout cell (F3) displays 840.

task_completion.description: Repair the formulas for David Lee (Row 5).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In row 5 (David Lee), the Commission cell (D5) displays 1540 and the Total Payout cell (F5) displays 2040.

task_completion.description: Repair the formulas for Frank Wilson (Row 7).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In row 7 (Frank Wilson), the Commission cell (D7) displays 1050 and the Total Payout cell (F7) displays 1550.

task_completion.description: Ensure actual formulas are used instead of hardcoded values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When one of the repaired cells (D3, D5, D7, F3, F5, or F7) is selected, the formula bar at the top shows a spreadsheet formula (e.g., containing 'IF' statements and cell references like C3 or E3) rather than a plain number.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Calc interface (clicking cells, typing in the formula bar, using fill handles) rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to modify the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the final calculated numbers into the cells.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows logical formulas (e.g., `=IF(...)`) being typed or copied into the corrupted cells, not just the raw expected numbers (840, 1540, etc.).

integrity.description: The agent modified the existing file rather than creating a new one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The intact rows (2, 4, 6, 8) remain unchanged and the file name remains 'sales_commissions.ods'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires repairing missing formulas in cells D3, D5, D7 (Commission) and F3, F5, F7 (Total Payout). Commission logic: ≤$10,000 = 5%, $10,001-$25,000 = 7%, >$25,000 = 10%. Payout logic: Commission + $500 bonus IF Status is 'Premium' AND Commission > $1,000. Expected values to verify: Row 3 (Bob Smith) Commission=$840, Payout=$840. Row 5 (David Lee) Commission=$1,540, Payout=$2,040. Row 7 (Frank Wilson) Commission=$1,050, Payout=$1,550. The agent must use relative formulas (e.g., `=IF(C3<=10000...`), not hardcoded values.
